title: Sidecar & Ambassador Patterns
summary: Sidecar containers for cross-cutting concerns, Ambassador for proxying, Dapr for portable microservices.

sections.content:
## Sidecar Pattern

The Sidecar pattern deploys a helper container alongside your main application container in the same pod. They share the same network namespace and optionally storage, but run as separate processes.

The sidecar handles **cross-cutting concerns** so the main app doesn't have to:
- Logging/metrics collection
- Service mesh proxy (Envoy)
- Secret rotation
- Config watching
- TLS termination

sections.title: Sidecar Container Pattern
sections.content:
graph LR
    subgraph Pod
        App[Main App\nPort 8080] <-->|localhost| Sidecar[Sidecar\nEnvoy Proxy]
        App <-->|shared volume| Logs[Log Collector\nFluentd]
    end
    Sidecar <-->|mTLS| Other[Other Service]
    Logs -->|ship logs| Loki[Loki]
    style App fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style Sidecar fill:#059669,color:#fff

## Ambassador Pattern

The Ambassador pattern uses a proxy (helper container or separate service) to represent the application to the outside world. It handles:
- Protocol translation
- Request routing
- Auth/authz at the edge
- Retries and circuit breaking on behalf of the app

```yaml
# Ambassador as a sidecar proxy
spec:
  containers:
  - name: my-app
    image: my-app:v1
    # communicates only with ambassador via localhost
  - name: ambassador
    image: envoyproxy/envoy:v1.28
    # handles all inbound/outbound traffic
    # - upstream service discovery
    # - retries, timeouts
    # - mTLS
```

**Key difference from Sidecar**: Ambassador specifically handles the communication proxy role, while Sidecar is broader (any co-located helper).

sections.title: Dapr Sidecar Architecture
sections.content:
graph LR
    subgraph Service A Pod
        AppA[App A] <-->|localhost:3500| DaprA[Dapr Sidecar]
    end
    subgraph Service B Pod
        AppB[App B] <-->|localhost:3500| DaprB[Dapr Sidecar]
    end
    DaprA <-->|mTLS| DaprB
    DaprA -->|pub/sub| Kafka[Kafka / RabbitMQ]
    DaprA -->|state| Redis[Redis / Cosmos]
    DaprA -->|secrets| Vault[Vault / K8s Secrets]
    style DaprA fill:#059689,color:#fff
    style DaprB fill:#059689,color:#fff

sections.title: Sidecar vs Ambassador vs Service Mesh

sections.question: What is the primary benefit of Dapr's sidecar approach for microservices?
options: It speeds up inter-service communication significantly
Applications talk to generic local APIs and Dapr handles the actual infrastructure — making services portable across brokers, state stores, and clouds
It replaces the need for a service mesh entirely
It compiles application code to native binaries for better performance
sections.explanation: Dapr exposes generic building block APIs on localhost. The actual implementation (Kafka vs RabbitMQ, Redis vs CosmosDB) is configured in Dapr components, not in application code. This makes services portable — you can switch infrastructure without code changes, and the same service runs identically on-premises or in any cloud.
